deduplication_summary.note: Input contains duplicate hypothesis_ids (TECH2-H003 through TECH2-H008 each appear twice with different content). Duplicate group descriptions specify which instance by title. For TECH2-H004, TECH2-H006, TECH2-H007, TECH2-H008: one instance is removed and one retained — consult group descriptions for disambiguation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PEMCertChainLib.decodeCert notBefore date tag validation logic error: condition (notBeforeTag != 0x17 && notBeforeTag == 0x18) simplifies to (notBeforeTag == 0x18), rejecting valid GeneralizedTime while accepting arbitrary invalid tags
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical boolean logic error in PEMCertChainLib.decodeCert notBefore tag validation. TECH-H001 is CRITICAL with the most precise technical analysis of the logic simplification and both DoS/bypass implications. Note: the TECH2-H003 instance here is the second occurrence titled 'Attestation bypass/DoS via crafted PCK cert notBefore tag acceptance'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TimelockTokenPool.withdraw(address,bytes) signature hash lacks domain separator (chain ID, contract address, nonce), enabling cross-chain and cross-instance replay
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical missing domain separator in TimelockTokenPool withdrawal signature hash keccak256(abi.encodePacked('Withdraw unlocked Taiko token to: ', _to)). ECON-H001 has the most detailed attack economics and clear replay scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AutomataDcapV3Attestation._verifyCertChain loop breaks at root CA pubkey hash match without validating root certificate's own expiry or self-signature
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the identical early loop termination at i=1 when ROOTCA_PUBKEY_HASH matches, skipping root cert validation. TECH-H003 provides the clearest technical analysis of the loop logic. Note: the TECH2-H003 instance here is the first occurrence titled 'Root Certificate Expiration and Revocation Bypass'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TimelockTokenPool.getMyGrantSummary costToWithdraw calculation divides amountUnlocked by 1e18 before multiplying by costPerToken, causing precision loss
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical division-before-multiplication precision loss in costToWithdraw. ECON2-H003 is rated HIGH (vs MEDIUM for others) with detailed economics. Note: the TECH2-H005 instance here is the first occurrence titled 'Precision Loss in TimelockTokenPool'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20Airdrop2.withdraw(address user) lacks access control, allowing anyone to force token delivery to any user during the withdrawal window
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the identical lack of msg.sender check in ERC20Airdrop2.withdraw(), enabling forced token receipt. ECON-H004 has the most detailed impact and economic analysis including tax implications.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AutomataDcapV3Attestation has no ownership transfer mechanism — owner set in constructor with no way to change it
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical missing ownership transfer in AutomataDcapV3Attestation. TECH-H006 has a more comprehensive listing of affected admin functions and deployment evidence.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PEMCertChainLib._findTcb allocates cpusvns array of size 16 but loop iterates 17 times, causing out-of-bounds revert if PCESVN OID is not the last entry
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical array bounds issue in _findTcb with cpusvns[16] OOB write. TECH-H007 has the most detailed technical analysis including both the OOB and the cpusvns gap issue. Note: the TECH2-H004 instance here is the first occurrence titled 'Array Out-of-Bounds DoS in PCK Certificate TCB Parsing'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SigVerifyLib.verifyES256Signature uses assert(success) after staticcall to ES256VERIFIER, consuming all remaining gas on failure
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats describe the identical assert(success) gas exhaustion after ES256VERIFIER staticcall. ECON2-H004 is rated HIGH with detailed prover griefing economics. Note: the TECH2-H007 instance here is the first occurrence titled 'Gas Exhaustion DoS in SigVerifyLib via assert()'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TaikoL2._calc1559BaseFee treats gasExcess==0 as an absorbing state, permanently disabling dynamic base fee if gasExcess reaches zero
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical gasExcess==0 absorbing state in _calc1559BaseFee. TECH-H009 has a clearer technical explanation of the code path and absorbing state behavior.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20Airdrop2 init() does not validate withdrawalWindow > 0, causing division-by-zero in getBalance() that permanently locks all claimed tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the identical missing zero-check for withdrawalWindow in init() leading to getBalance() division-by-zero. ECON-H008 is rated HIGH with detailed economic impact. Note: the TECH2-H006 instance here is the first occurrence titled 'Division by Zero DoS in ERC20Airdrop2'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded gas consumption in DCAP attestation verification pipeline (Base64/ASN.1/signature verification on untrusted inputs) enables gas-griefing DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same general gas-DoS vector through expensive attestation verification on untrusted inputs without early cheap prefilters. TECH-H013 provides more detailed analysis of the specific heavy operations involved.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: verifyParsedQuote accepts pre-parsed struct bypassing raw quote binary validation from parseInput; reconstructed signedQuoteData via abi.encodePacked may differ from canonical format
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the identical dual-entry-path vulnerability where verifyParsedQuote bypasses parseInput structural checks and reconstructs signedQuoteData. ECON2-H014 has the most comprehensive analysis of the specific validation gaps and attack economics. Note: the TECH2-H005 instance here is the second occurrence titled 'Parsed-quote API may enable quote format confusion'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20Airdrop2.claim() replay protection is per-(user,amount) leaf hash, but claimedAmount is per-user additive, allowing multiple claims from different merkle leaves for the same user
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical mismatch between per-leaf replay protection and per-user additive accounting in ERC20Airdrop2. TECH-H015 focuses more precisely on the specific code-level mismatch between _verifyClaim uniqueness and claimedAmount accumulation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TaikoL2._calcPublicInputHash ring-buffer may produce inconsistent hashes at low block numbers due to genesis initialization edge cases
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical ring-buffer initialization edge case at genesis/low block heights that could cause permanent publicInputHash mismatch. TECH-H017 has more detailed analysis of the specific block.number==0/1 handling.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PEMCertChainLib.splitCertificateChain/_removeHeadersAndFooters PEM parsing boundary issues allowing misaligned certificate extraction
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical PEM header/footer parsing boundary issue in splitCertificateChain. OPS-H018 has more detailed analysis of the indexOf header/footer matching vulnerability. Note: the TECH2-H008 instance here is the second occurrence titled 'Certificate-chain parsing may accept partial/misaligned PEM segments'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable uninitialized customConfig (all zeros) causes Lib1559Math.basefee to revert, halting the L2 chain if setConfigAndExcess is not called atomically with upgrade
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the identical uninitialized customConfig causing chain halt. ECON-H017 is rated CRITICAL with clearer description of the non-atomic upgrade scenario and the chain-halt mechanism.
